title: Define a basic Money struct
scenario: The system needs to represent monetary amounts in integer minor units. To prevent bugs, we must group the numeric amount and the currency type together into a single domain type instead of passing loose primitives around.
instructions: Define a public Money struct with private amount (u64 minor units) and currency (Currency) fields.

hints.body: The fields should be private: amount: u64 and currency: Currency. Do not add pub before them.
A canonical solution defines the Money struct with private fields.

completionExplanation: The Money struct keeps the amount and currency together as one domain value. Private fields prevent callers from mutating the representation directly, which gives later constructors and methods a single place to preserve invariants.
